From the camera's viewpoint, the cabinet is in which direction relative to the curtain?

A.  above
B.  front-right
C.  back-right
D.  left

From the current camera perspective, what is the spatial relationship of the cabinet to the night stand?

A.  below
B.  right
C.  above
D.  back-right

In the current image, is the desk unoccluded, occluded by another object, or not visible? Here, 'occluded' means blocked by another object; being partly outside the image frame does not count as occlusion.

A.  not occluded
B.  occluded
C.  not visible

Estimate the approximate shortest distance between the counter and the sofa, measured from their closest points.

A.  far (>3.3m)
B.  very close (<1.0m)
C.  close (1.0-2.0m)
D.  moderate (2.0-3.3m)

What is the approximate shortest distance between the counter and the shelves, measured from their closest points?

A.  moderate (2.0-3.3m)
B.  close (1.0-2.0m)
C.  far (>3.3m)
D.  very close (<1.0m)

Measured from the closest points of each object, what is the approximate shortest distance between the pillow and the night stand?

A.  far (>3.3m)
B.  very close (<1.0m)
C.  moderate (2.0-3.3m)
D.  close (1.0-2.0m)

Measured from the closest points of each object, what is the approximate shortest distance between the night stand and the cabinet?

A.  moderate (2.0-3.3m)
B.  close (1.0-2.0m)
C.  very close (<1.0m)
D.  far (>3.3m)

Imagine you are the night stand and facing toward the curtain. From your perspective, in which direction is the cabinet?

A.  below
B.  right
C.  above
D.  front-right

If you were the books, looking toward the desk, where would the chair be?

A.  front-left
B.  below
C.  back-left
D.  right

If you were the night stand, looking toward the pillow, where would the bed be?

A.  back-right
B.  front
C.  right
D.  above

If you were the cabinet, looking toward the curtain, where would the night stand be?

A.  above
B.  back-left
C.  front-left
D.  below

Imagine you are the shelves and facing toward the counter. From your perspective, in which direction is the sofa?

A.  above
B.  right
C.  front
D.  front-left

If you were the sofa, looking toward the counter, where would the shelves be?

A.  right
B.  back
C.  below
D.  left

In this image the camera faces northwest. Viewed from above on the room's layout, the sofa is in which cardinal direction relative to the counter?

A.  north
B.  east
C.  northwest
D.  southwest

The camera is facing west in this scene. On the room's floor plan, in which cardinal direction is the curtain from the cabinet?

A.  southeast
B.  west
C.  north
D.  south

The camera is facing south in this scene. On the room's floor plan, in which cardinal direction is the bed from the night stand?

A.  southeast
B.  west
C.  north
D.  northeast

A.  west
B.  north
C.  northwest
D.  south

A.  east
B.  northwest
C.  southwest
D.  west

A.  east
B.  south
C.  northwest
D.  southwest

From the camera's perspective, if the bed is moved backward (toward the camera) by 0.5m, which best describes the pillow: not occluded, occluded, or not visible? Here, 'occluded' means blocked by another object; being partly outside the image frame does not count as occlusion.

A.  not occluded
B.  occluded
C.  not visible

After the camera moves right by 2m without changing its viewing direction, which best describes the night stand: not occluded, occluded, or not visible? Here, 'occluded' means blocked by another object; being partly outside the image frame does not count as occlusion.

A.  not occluded
B.  occluded
C.  not visible

If the camera translates right by 2m while keeping its intrinsics and orientation unchanged, what is the occlusion status of the table? Here, 'occluded' means blocked by another object; being partly outside the image frame does not count as occlusion.

A.  not visible
B.  not occluded
C.  occluded

If the camera translates right by 2m while keeping its intrinsics and orientation unchanged, what is the occlusion status of the cabinet? Here, 'occluded' means blocked by another object; being partly outside the image frame does not count as occlusion.

A.  occluded
B.  not occluded
C.  not visible

If the camera translates right by 2m while keeping its intrinsics and orientation unchanged, what is the occlusion status of the night stand? Here, 'occluded' means blocked by another object; being partly outside the image frame does not count as occlusion.

A.  not occluded
B.  occluded
C.  not visible

A.  occluded
B.  not occluded
C.  not visible

A.  not visible

Imagine the room was originally built rotated 90 degrees clockwise (as seen from above). With all inter-object relationships intact and the camera at its original pose, from the camera's perspective, what is the direction of the desk from the chair?

A.  back-right
B.  front-left
C.  front-right
D.  front

A.  back-left
B.  left
C.  back-right
D.  front-right
